name: North Macedonia
summary: North Macedonia is a newer NATO member (joined 2020) currently focused on aligning its intelligence and legal frameworks with EU standards. In 2024-2025, the security landscape is defined by countering foreign hybrid warfare aimed at ethnic polarization and institutional trust. The country is in the final stages of a comprehensive security sector reform while managing the regional stability challenges of the Western Balkans.

politicalStability.note: Stable but polarized political environment; ongoing efforts to combat high-level corruption and ensure ethnic cohesion.

economicDirection.note: Steady growth driven by EU integration prospects and infrastructure projects, though hampered by regional instability.

investmentAttractiveness.note: Attractive for manufacturing and automotive parts due to low costs and EU proximity; corruption remains a concern.

geopoliticalRisk.note: Vulnerable to Russian-led disinformation and regional ethnic tensions; NATO membership provides a critical safety net.

educationQuality.note: Standardized public system; facing 'brain drain' of skilled youth to Western Europe.

healthcareQuality.note: Public system in need of modernization; significant disparity between urban and rural care.

technologyInvestment.note: Growing IT outsourcing sector; focus on digitalizing government services under EU guidance.

relationships.summary: Strongest security ally; providing significant support for military modernization and judicial reform.
Relations have improved significantly since the Prespa Agreement; Greece is now a key economic partner and EU advocate.
Tense relations due to historical and linguistic disputes that have occasionally delayed MKD's EU accession process.
Close regional ally; joint focus on EU integration and regional stability in the Western Balkans.

perspectives.view: Our priority is professionalizing our agencies and rooting out foreign influence that seeks to divide our multi-ethnic society.
The EU path feels slow; we need real jobs and less corruption, otherwise more of my friends will move to Germany.
The costs are low and NATO provides security, but the slow pace of legal reform makes long-term projects risky.

historicalContext.summary: Part of Yugoslavia until its peaceful secession in 1991, the country has navigated complex ethnic tensions (culminating in the 2001 Ohrid Agreement) and a long-running name dispute with Greece that was resolved in 2018, paving the way for NATO and EU integration.

keyEvents.event: Ohrid Framework Agreement ends ethnic conflict.
Prespa Agreement with Greece resolves name dispute.
Official name change to Republic of North Macedonia.
Launch of the 2024-2025 Defence Strategy focused on asymmetric threats.

strengths: Competitive labor costs and tax incentives for FDI
Strategic location on European transport corridors
NATO security guarantee provides regional stability
risks: High levels of perceived corruption in the judiciary
Susceptibility to foreign-backed disinformation and polarization
Significant brain drain and labor shortages in technical fields
Slow pace of EU accession negotiations
